results.title: Financial Operations Analyst, VPP P&L - RevOps Careers
highlights:
Financial Operations Analyst, VPP P&L - RevOps Careers

# Financial Operations Analyst, VPP P&L

Hybrid Full Time Plano, Texas, United States NRG Energy

NRG is seeking a Financial Operations Analyst on the Virtual Power Plant (VPP) team, who is passionate about facilitating, developing and enhancing products and solutions that provide load flexibility to the Grid. This position requires an analytical, organized, structured, detail-oriented, curious, creative and proactive candidate.

## Requirements

- Post a Job
- Build a deep understanding of the market landscape
- Create reporting and analytical frameworks to generate actionable insights from VPP enrollment, maintenance, and performance data
- Analyze portfolio performance to identify trends, key drivers, risks, and growth opportunities
- Develop and maintain an integrated view of core performance metrics across the active VPP product portfolio
- Conduct research and generate insights, use-cases, and industry analyses to address potential future opportunities
- Leverage AI-powered tools and analytics platforms to connect data points, generate deeper insights and automate routine analysis where possible
- Support forecasting, scenario modeling and strategic business reviews by utilizing advanced analytics techniques
- Provide in depth analysis with interpretive thinking to define problems, develop innovative solutions, and influence strategic product portfolio decisions
- Identify and communicate risk and impacts of proposed solutions along with risk mitigation options considering business implications of the application of the product concept
- Provide support on requested tasks, organize tasks, communicate and proactively report performance and develop next steps
- Understand financial impacts of partner agreements and ensure payments are accurate between NRG and our partners

## Benefits

- Dynamic work environment
- Unified and inclusive culture
- Strong sense of belonging
- Professional development opportunities
- Competitive salary and benefits

Tagged as: Financial Modeling

To apply for this job please visit careers.nrgenergy.com.

---

NRG. • United Kingdom

[...]
Fully Remote | Outside IR35 | Immediate Start
[...]
I am working with an award winning UK business operating at a significant scale with a large turnover, stable leadership and exciting project pipeline. They pride themselves on being honest, hard working and have high standards. This company really value fast paced people who quite simply - get things done.
[...]
They are looking for a genuinely strong Data Engineer to come in and finish a Microsoft Fabric implementation properly. Not manage it. Not sit in meetings all day. Not produce PowerPoints. We need someone who can roll their sleeves up and do the work.
[...]
This role has immediate
[...]
start tomorrow.
[...]
They have a platform built that has ingested their ERP data source and in reality it is 90% there. The product is functional but they want it take to the next level. They want an experienced engineer who can come in, assess the product quickly, fix it and move it forward.
[...]
What You’ll Be Doing
[...]
- Complete and stabilise the ERP data ingestion
- Fix and tidy the Fabric architecture
- Ensure proper Gold-layer data quality and modelling
- Improve reliability/performance of existing pipelines
- Work alongside our Senior Data Analyst and internal development team
[...]
Integrate two additional data sources. Both are achievable integrations and their internal dev team can support where needed.
[...]
You must be hands-on and technically strong.
[...]
- Strong Microsoft Fabric experience
- Data Engineering background
- SQL expertise
- API ingestion/integration experience
- PySpark / Python
- Data modelling
- Experience working with ERP style datasets
- Ability to work independently and move quickly
[...]
This is an opportunity for the right engineer to come in, make a visible impact quickly, and own the delivery properly.

results.title: Graduate Rotational Analyst - Risk and M&A | Wfhverse
highlights:
# Graduate Rotational Analyst - Risk and M&A
[...]
Full-time
[...]
NRG Energy is focused on creating a smarter, cleaner, and more connected future in the energy sector. They are seeking a Graduate Rotational Analyst for their Graduate Rotation Program, which involves two one-year rotations in different departments, aimed at developing future leaders through hands-on experience in risk management and M&A. The role includes various analytical responsibilities and collaboration across teams to support the company's operations and strategic initiatives. Responsibilities Production of Daily/Monthly P&L/Position reports Provide insight into positions and risk of the portfolio Validate end of day results in the risk systems Leverage problem solving and analytical skills to enhance and automate numerous risk processes Collaborate effectively within the Risk Management team and other departments on issues/projects Support reporting requests from NRG’s internal and external auditors Work closely with IT on risk system improvements Ad hoc requests for trading/management Provide analytical support on a variety of M&A transactions, strategy projects and venture capital investments Develop, maintain and/or review complex financial models (including valuation and sensitivity analyses) to support decision making for investments and M&A activities Ensure that all assumptions and modeling techniques are appropriate and reasonable and confirm that model accurately reflects the terms and conditions contained in key project documentation Work closely with business units, tax, accounting, various technical teams and advisors to prepare detailed forecast of profit and loss, cash flows and balance sheet for acquisition or investment targets Monitor competitive landscape, be able to assess implications for NRG and communicate salient points to the team and management as needed Assist in determination of key value drivers of NRG’s corporate valuation Assist in evaluation of a variety of financial structures necessary to support contemplated transactions Prepare recommendations to be presented to senior management Coordinate and manage due diligence for contemplated transactions Assist in providing data for NRG earnings releases and other corporate functions, such as meetings of the Board of Directors and executive leadership Create and maintain reports summarizing the ongoing performance of venture capital portfolio and strategic investments Assist in providing regular updates of key corporate valuation metrics including performing financial analysis to determine present and future financial performance Complete ad-hoc projects and create reports as needed Skills Degree discipline: Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Mathematics, Information Systems, Computer Science Must have graduated no longer than 6 months from program start Must have a 3.3 or higher GPA Must have legal authorization to work in the US on a full-time basis without sponsorship Benefits Medical Vision Dental 401K Flexible spending accounts Company Overview NRG Energy is an integrated power generation and supply firm that offers offsite solar, wind power, and smart grid retail services. It was founded in 1989, and is headquartered in Houston, Texas, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is http://www.nrg.com/.

highlights:
# Senior AI/ML Engineer
[...]
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
[...]
Develop AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector
[...]
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
[...]
Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development
[...]
Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
[...]
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
[...]
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
[...]
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
[...]
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
[...]
- Develop frameworks for
[...]
- Support database design and optimization, including writing efficient queries for large-scale data processing and retrieval.
[...]
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
[...]
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, or related field (PhD preferred).
[...]
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
[...]
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
[...]
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
[...]
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
[...]
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
[...]
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
[...]
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
[...]
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
[...]
- Nice to have: experience in the utility, energy, or power sector and strong Databricks expertise.
[...]

results.title: Energy Data Strategist (Staff/ Sr Staff) | Hirro
highlights:
Energy Data Strategist (Staff/ Sr Staff) | Hirro
[...]
# Energy Data Strategist (Staff/ Sr Staff)
[...]
Equilibrium Energy is a team of technologists, power market experts, and AI pioneers reimagining how the world’s most critical industry operates. We’re building a first-of-its-kind AI operating system for the power sector, uniting cutting-edge science with real-world purpose to enable a cleaner, more resilient energy future. At EQ, you’ll join a tight-knit group of brilliant, curious, and adventurous people who bring the same energy to collaboration as they do to innovation.
[...]
Equilibrium Energy is a well-funded, Series B clean energy startup backed by some of the most prominent institutional investors in climate. New colleagues will share our vision that a next-generation energy company must be built from the ground up on deep industry expertise combined with an unwavering commitment to modern digital approaches. We’re looking for collaborative, talented, passionate and resourceful folks to join our team and help us lay the foundation for our important mission and ambitious plan.
[...]
Data is central to the success of the Equilibrium Energy mission. The data managed by the Data Services team is incredibly robust ranging from telemetry outputs to inputs into machine learning models and includes public, proprietary and private data points. All of this needs to be centralized, synthesized and governed to ensure its secure usability across our various teams. The role of the Energy Analyst is to advance our data system and platform so it will empower our teams to better utilize our market data warehouse. The Energy Data Strategist will do this by applying domain and market expertise to advance our raw data into explorable, understandable data views and processes. The ideal candidate will have a passion for energy markets, data engineering and analytics.
[...]
- Engineering and Analytics

- Data Support and Quality Assurance

- The minimum qualifications you’ll need

- Nice to have additional skills
[...]
Equilibrium is composed of deeply knowledgeable industry experts across all our functions, with decades of experience in energy-specific commercial structuring, power systems engineering, machine learning, computational research, operations research, distributed and compute-intensive infrastructure, and modern software & ML engineering. Our experience in the space means we’ve previously built versions of nearly every technical component of our platform. We are now designing them better, and combining them in a holistic and novel way, to achieve global scale and climate impact. We pride ourselves on our deeply empathetic & collaborative culture, honest and direct but respectful communication, and our balanced, flexible, and remote-first work environment.

results.title: NRG Advances AI Economy, Customer Affordability, and Grid Strength | NRG Energy posted on the topic | LinkedIn
highlights:
NRG Advances AI Economy, Customer Affordability, and Grid Strength | NRG Energy posted on the topic | LinkedIn
[...]
# NRG Advances AI Economy, Customer Affordability, and Grid Strength
[...]
Powering the AI economy. Driving customer affordability. Strengthening the grid. NRG is advancing all three. In a recent conversation with Fortune, CEO Robert Gaudette explains how we're scaling Bring Your Own Power and Virtual Power Plants to deliver for customers, hyperscalers, and the grid. Read the story: https://bit.ly/4nEYtz2
[...]
New NRG Energy CEO leans into growth with ‘bring your own power' for the AI boom and ‘virtual power plants' | Fortune fortune.com
[...]
Global Director, Energy & Utilities ·
[...]
| Data & AI Strategy for the Energy
[...]
What if the electric grid could sense, reason, and adapt in real time? That's the promise of the "cognitive grid": an AI-enabled way of working that helps utilities coordinate infrastructure, customer behavior, and large loads as part of one connected system. It's not a single platform or a piece of software. It's an operational shift that pairs machine intelligence with human judgment. In this sixth article in Guidehouse's AI-powered energy series, our experts show what a cognitive grid looks like in practice, including a day-in-the-life scenario and five actions to take. It's a timely read for any utility leaders working to operationalize intelligence.
[...]
AI agents are essential for modern utilities facing surging demand and extreme weather. Learn how intelligent augmentation transforms grid operations, breaks down data silos, and improves reliability.
[...]

Data Scientist for Nuclear Power Applications Postdoctoral Researcher

The Idaho National Laboratory (INL) is seeking a well-qualified Postdoctoral Research Associate in Data Science for the Department of Plant Optimization. The ideal candidate should possess a robust technical background and practical experience with large language models (LLMs).
[...]
doctoral Researcher will have the following key responsibilities:
[...]
- Develop, fine-tune, and optimize LLMs.
- Design and implement efficient retrieval-augmented generation systems.
- Create advanced prompt engineering strategies to enhance model performance and utility.
- Demonstrate expertise in embedding techniques, with a clear understanding of their strengths, weaknesses, and applicability to various use cases.

[...]

to export control restrictions per the Department

Energy. For this reason, you must be either a US Citizen or a US Green Card holder to qualify for the job.

The ideal candidate will possess:

Idaho National Laboratory

INL is a science-based, applied engineering national laboratory dedicated to supporting the U.S. Department of Energy’s mission in nuclear energy research, science, and national defense. With more than 6,300 scientists, researchers, and support staff, the laboratory works with national and international governments, universities and industry partners to change the world’s energy future and secure our nation’s critical infrastructure.

Our mission is to discover, demonstrate and secure innovative nuclear energy solutions, other clean energy options and critical infrastructure.

results.title: NDA, renewable energy assets | AI Developer | CareerSync
highlights:
NDA, renewable energy assets | AI Developer | CareerSync

CareerSync.← All Jobs

# NDA, renewable energy assets | AI Developer

Posted Jun 7, 2026

On behalf of NDA, renewable energy assets, SD Solutions is looking for a talented AI Developer to build high-performance, low-latency web applications.

SD Solutions is a staffing company operating globally. Contact us to get more details about the benefits we offer.

You will design, build, and ship end-to-end AI features: from LangChain/LangGraph orchestration logic on the backend to the interfaces that expose those capabilities to our users.

### Responsibilities:

Backend / AI-based features - must have:

- Design, implement, and own multi-step agentic flows using LangChain, LangGraph, and similar.
- Build tools, agents, and chains that connect to live data sources (APIs, databases, time-series feeds)
- Integrate LLMs (Claude, GPT, Gemini, etc') into reliable, observable pipelines
- Own prompt engineering, context management, and output validation
- Experience working in large-scale and big data environments
- Deploy and monitor AI services on AWS or Azure
- Instrument flows with logging and tracing so we can debug and improve them

Frontend - big advantage:

- Build React interfaces that surface AI outputs: summaries, alerts, recommendations, and interactive co-pilot features
- Connect frontend components to streaming AI responses
- Write clean, maintainable TypeScript code

### Requirements:

- 6+ years of full-stack development experience
- Hands-on, production-grade experience with LangChain and LangGraph or similar agentic orchestration in production, not just tutorials
- Strong Python for AI/backend work and TypeScript/React for frontend
- Experience integrating LLMs via API (Bedrock, GCP Agent Platform, or similar)
- Familiarity with vector stores, RAG pipelines, and structured output parsing
- Comfort working in a fast-moving startup environment where you own your features end-to-end

Nice to Have

- Experience in energy, IoT, or industrial data domains
- Experience with data pipelines such as Lambda/Medallion with data integrity validations
- Familiarity with LangFuse or other LLM observability tools
- Experience with MongoDB Atlas, ClickHouse, and time-series data

### About the company:

NDA, renewable energy assets, is your centralized view into the status and needs of your renewable energy assets. Collect, store, and learn from your digital assets in a single, centralized place, using the latest real-time monitoring and machine learning algorithms, to optimize your performance and reduce consumption, at scale.

By applying for this position, you agree to the terms outlined in our Privacy Policy. Please take a moment to review our Privacy Policy https://sd-solutions.breezy.hr/privacy-notice, and make sure you understand its contents. If you have any questions or concerns regarding our Privacy Policy, please feel free to contact us.

Apply for This Job

results.title: Grid Integration Engineer - Battery Storage Job Opening - Crains Cleveland
highlights:
Conduct steady-state transmission system analysis using TARA or related software to evaluate large load and data center interconnection opportunities, identify network upgrade risks, and assess mitigation pathways. Support technical analysis associated with large load interconnection and transmission service requests across U.S. markets, with emphasis on ERCOT, MISO, SPP, and PJM. Review utility, ISO, and consultant study results to determine feasibility, upgrade exposure, and schedule risk for prospective projects. Develop data-driven tools, screening methods, and dashboards to improve interconnection evaluation, portfolio prioritization, and decision-making. Use Python to automate data processing, study workflows, case management, and repetitive analytical tasks. Leverage AI-driven tools such as Perplexity and GitHub Copilot, where appropriate, to improve workflow speed, document review, research efficiency, code development, data organization, and technical screening. Participate in interconnection scoping meetings and other technical discussions with utilities, consultants, and grid authorities. Monitor and assess transmission planning, queue reform, and large load integration developments that affect project strategy in key markets. Support technical due diligence on transmission and interconnection constraints for prospective development opportunities. Provide technical guidance to internal teams on transmission and interconnection considerations, including upgrade risk, study assumptions, and market-specific process requirements. Attend and contribute to stakeholder meetings and calls in ERCOT, MISO, SPP, and PJM, especially those related to data centers, large load integration, interconnection policy, queue management, and transmission planning. Track stakeholder initiatives, tariff changes, and policy developments related to large load interconnection and communicate key implications to internal stakeholders. Skills & Qualifications
[...]
B.S. in Electrical Engineering required; M.S. in Electrical Engineering preferred, especially with a focus in Power Systems or Energy Systems. 3+ years of professional experience in the power and energy industry with a focus on transmission, interconnection, or power systems analysis. Strong background in power systems analysis, transmission fundamentals, and study interpretation. Demonstrated proficiency with TARA for transmission system analysis or strong adjacent experience with comparable tools. Familiarity with PSS/E for load modeling or related transmission analysis work is preferred. Strong Python skills for automation, data analysis, and workflow improvement. Familiarity with AI-driven tools, including Perplexity and GitHub Copilot, that can improve engineering productivity, technical research, coding efficiency, data handling, and analytical workflow speed. Working knowledge of stakeholder processes, planning structures, and market rules in ERCOT, MISO, SPP, and PJM. Ability to represent the company professionally in stakeholder calls and technical meetings involving large load and data center integration topics. Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills. Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams in a fast-moving development environment. Proficiency with Microsoft Office products, especially Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. Experience or demonstrated interest in the evolving regulatory and market environment around large load interconnection, including data center growth, queue pressures, and stakeholder reforms in organized markets, is highly desirable.

# Senior AI and ML Scientists (multiple positions)
[...]
Join our established AI team, bringing scientific depth and rigor to drive innovation across diverse specializations, from agentic AI to industrial vision and signal machine learning, and ensure robust, uncertainty-aware production systems.
[...]
We are seeking Senior AI and ML Scientists across several critical specialisations to bring scientific depth to our established AI organization. These roles are pivotal in raising evaluation rigor, mentoring across the team, and taking ownership of the specific modalities our industrial use cases demand.
[...]
You will apply scientific rigor across all stages of AI delivery, from initial problem framing through to evaluation design. This involves technical deep dives within your chosen specialization, contributing to our collective knowledge, and raising the bar across the team. A key aspect is mentoring data scientists and engineers, and actively moving research into production through collaboration with MLOps, architecture, and pod engineers. You will also contribute to establishing uncertainty quantification standards, understanding how prediction confidence and calibration affect safe automation thresholds, and promoting software engineering discipline in scientific work, including version-controlled experiments, peer-reviewed code, and production-ready implementations.
[...]
agentic AI at

PhD in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Statistics, or related field — or equivalent demonstrated depth.
[...]
Track record of moving research into production, not just publishing.

Deep working knowledge of uncertainty quantification, calibration, and out-of-distribution behaviour in autonomous AI.

Background in industrial, energy, or

Demonstrated work on evaluation methodology for probabilistic and non-deterministic outputs.

-learning applied
[...]
or signal data.

Track 4: Senior Computer Vision Engineer / Scientist
[...]
Our ambition is clear: to become the world’s first AI‑native exploration and production company.
[...]
AI-native E&P
[...]
We didn't discover AI last year. For a decade, we've been building the data foundation and interoperable ecosystem that now lets us scale AI across some of the most complex operations in our industry.
[...]
Our ambition is simple to state and hard to deliver: a 3x productivity improvement. Getting there demands more than incremental gains, it requires a fundamental redesign of how work gets done. You get there by redesigning how decisions are made, how work is planned, and how assets are run, with AI agents as native participants in the operating model. And that's why we're looking for ambitious, driven AI professionals who want to help rewrite how a large industrial company operates.

# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data

- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and
[...]
to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology,
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python

- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence

Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: NRG’s new CEO has a plan to power the AI boom—and lower your energy bill
highlights:
# NRG’s new CEO has a plan to power the AI boom—and lower your energy bill
[...]
NRG’s new CEO is riding the AI wave by building gas plants for hyperscalers while rolling out smart grid tech and virtual power plants—aiming to cut your energy bill in the process.
[...]
Robert Gaudette took over NRG Energy at the end of April 2026.
[...]
Build gas-fired power plants for AI data centers and expand demand-response programs.
[...]
“Virtual power plants” and smart grid tech to optimize energy use and lower costs for consumers.
[...]
Robert Gaudette officially took over as CEO of NRG Energy at the end of April 2026, stepping into a role that places him at the intersection of two powerful trends: the insatiable energy appetite of AI and the growing demand for affordable, reliable electricity for everyday consumers. Gaudette’s strategy is not just about building more power plants—it’s about building smarter ones.
[...]
“We’re going to ride the AI wave,” Gaudette said, signaling a clear shift toward meeting the “bespoke desires and needs” of hyperscalers—the tech giants like Google, Amazon, and Microsoft that are racing to expand their AI infrastructure. But his vision goes far beyond simply feeding the data center beast.
[...]
Gaudette’s plan has two distinct but interconnected tracks. The first is straightforward: NRG is rapidly building new gas-fired power plants to meet the immediate, massive energy demands of AI data centers. These facilities are designed to provide the reliable, around-the-clock power that hyperscalers require to keep their servers running.
[...]
The second track is where the plan gets innovative—and where consumers stand to benefit. NRG is leaning heavily into demand-response programs and so-called “virtual power plants.” These systems use smart technology to turn the U.S. power grid from a “dumb” one-way street into a dynamic, two-way network. By convincing both industrial users and residential customers to hand over control of their thermostats to AI during peak demand hours, NRG can reduce strain on the grid and avoid firing up expensive, polluting backup plants.
[...]
For residential customers, the promise is simple: allow NRG’s AI to adjust your thermostat during peak times, and you could see a noticeable drop in your monthly bill. For industrial users, the incentives are even larger, with potential savings running into the millions. Gaudette has framed this as a win-win: “We’re not just building power plants; we’re building a smarter, more efficient energy ecosystem.”
[...]
What we know: Gaudette has committed to building new gas plants for AI data centers. NRG is actively expanding its demand-response and virtual power plant programs. The company believes these initiatives can lower costs for consumers.
[...]
For homeowners, the key takeaway is to watch for NRG’s demand-response programs in your area. If you have a smart thermostat, you may soon be eligible for incentives that lower your bill. For investors, NRG’
[...]
a hedge: exposure to the AI boom through gas plants, and a long-term play on
[...]
, Gaudette’
[...]
is a case study in how to balance
[...]
the AI era.
[...]
NRG is expanding demand-response programs and virtual power plants that allow AI to optimize energy use during peak hours. By reducing strain on the grid, the company can avoid costly backup power and pass savings on to consumers who participate.
[...]
A virtual power plant is a network of distributed energy resources—like smart thermostats, solar panels, and batteries—that can be controlled remotely to balance supply and demand. NRG uses AI to manage these resources, returning excess renewable energy to the grid and lowering costs.
[...]
NRG has not yet announced a specific rollout timeline. The company is expected to launch pilot programs in select markets in the coming months. Check NRG’s website or contact customer service for updates on availability in your region.

results.title: New NRG CEO leans into growth with ‘bring your own power’ for the AI boom and ‘virtual power plants’ - WDC TV NEWS
highlights:
New NRG CEO leans into growth with ‘bring your own power’ for the AI boom and ‘virtual power plants’ - WDC TV NEWS
[...]
Robert Gaudette took over as the new CEO of power and electricity giant NRG Energy at the end of April ready to ride the AI wave and build the “bespoke desires and needs” of hyperscalers nationwide.
[...]
But NRG isn’t only in the business of rapidly building new power plants—primarily gas-fired facilities—to satiate the hunger of data centers. NRG also is leaning into the burgeoning business of turning the United States’ dumb power grid smart with better technology and efficiency.
[...]
That’s where demand-response programs and so-called “virtual power plants” come into play, convincing both industrial users and residential customers to turn their thermostats over to AI at times of peak demand—and returning excess renewable energy back to the grid if available—to save energy and keep energy prices lower, essentially acting as a de facto power plant.
[...]
NRG (No. 153 on the Fortune 500) represents a tale of two strategies—rapid power growth to satisfy demand and smarter grid efficiency to help solve the rising utility bill woes that are triggering AI backlashes nationwide.
[...]
“We’ve barely started the first inning from a VPP-virtual power plant perspective,” Gaudette told Fortune in a sit-down interview. “Where we are today is now we have an affordability issue. Now we have a need. You have the combination of technology, willingness, and the economic desire to find a way to mobilize the power of the consumer to make a difference.”
[...]
Gaudette is leaning into those two acronyms, VPP and BYOP—bring your own power—paid for by hyperscalers and built by NRG. “NRG is uniquely positioned with two attacks on how we solve the challenges,” he said.
[...]
NRG is pushing for AI complexes in Texas, but Gaudette insists there are no limitations. New AI deals are expected later this year.
[...]
go anywhere in the country
[...]
t been specific about where
[...]
Gaudette said
[...]
Likewise, NRG recently introduced a residential VPP program with its Vivint smart home and Reliant retail electricity businesses. NRG gives away smart doorbells and thermostats for contracted participants.
[...]
“We’ve never harnessed the power of the load. We have never gone out of our way to help those people who are using the electricity to use it smarter, to use less of it, and to be more thoughtful about how they do it,” Gaudette said. “It’s hard, and there hasn’t been technology to do it. We’re in a different place now.”
[...]
,” Gaudette
[...]
“What technology has done is now you can take that same thought process and apply it across a million homes. That’s the big change.”
[...]
But those are seasonal factors. What Wall Street wants is for NRG to start announcing some big AI deals and build those 5.4 gigawatts of power—and more.
[...]
After all, NRG aimed to complete 20 megawatts of VPP power last year. Instead, it achieved 200 megawatts. NRG recently announced plans to add more than 1 gigawatt of battery storage power to its systems. And NRG is currently building about 1.5 gigawatts of gas-fired power plants at three locations around the Houston area. NRG also forecasts 2 gigawatts of expansion and efficiency projects in the Northeast and Midwest.
[...]
### A Kennedy, Kellyanne Conway’s ex-husband and a former Palantir data scientist debated AI regulation. Welcome to the Manhattan primary

We have never gone out of our way to help those people who are using the electricity to use it smarter, to use less of it, and to be more thoughtful about how they do it,” Gaudette said. “It’s hard, and there hasn’t been technology to do it. We’re in a different place now.”
[...]
“We have entire teams that work directly with big customers like that, and we’ve been doing that for a decade,” Gaudette said. “It’s not just around traditional-demand response, but also, ‘Just maybe you should schedule your maintenance outage for next weekend, because it looks like it’s going be very hot and prices are going to be very high.’

After all, NRG aimed to complete 20 megawatts of VPP power last year. Instead, it achieved 200 megawatts. NRG recently announced plans to add more than 1 gigawatt of battery storage power to its systems. And NRG is currently building about 1.5 gigawatts of gas-fired power plants at three locations around the Houston area. NRG also forecasts 2 gigawatts of expansion and efficiency projects in the Northeast and Midwest.

results.title: Lead Generative AI Specialist | Zenvok
highlights:
Lead Generative AI Specialist | Zenvok
[...]
# Lead Generative AI Specialist
[...]
Are you passionate about improving capabilities, efficiency and performance? Are you interested in responsible for supporting the business owners to lean the process? Join our Digital Technology Team! Our team provides industry-leading products and services that optimize the production and processing of energy. You will contribute to our future as an AI player in the Energy Transformation industry. Partner with the best As a Generative AI Specialist, you will work in teams providing smart solution and addressing problems in a commercial technology and consultancy development environment. In this role, you will contribute to the development and deployment of modern generative AI models, techniques and operational research to leverage the power of data to solve complex problems and push the boundaries of what is possible in the ever-evolving tech landscape. As a Lead Generative AI Specialist, you will be responsible for: Designing and developing of generative AI models, including but not limited to LLMs, VLMs, GANs and Multimodal LLMs. Conducting and driving forward innovative research in the field of generative AI, leading the team in exploring new methodologies and technologies. Working closely with cross-functional teams to integrate new generative models into existing AI systems, ensuring seamless integration and optimizing performance. Developing and testing software components according to customer and technical requirements. Preparing comprehensive reports and presentations on the impact of generative AI initiatives on the business. Fuel your passion T o be successful in this role you will have: Master's degree in computer science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field with a specialization in generative models and proven experience in the development and deployment of generative AI models. PhD is preferred Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work across different disciplines. Proven experience in software programming languages, with a focus on Python, including AI libraries eg PyTorch, TensorFlow, Transformer architecture. Demonstrated skill at function in a team setting Demonstrated awareness of critical thinking and problem solving methods Demonstrated awareness of presentation and influencing skills Ability to analyze and resolve problems through innovation and creative solutions. Natural communication skills, with an ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. Self-motivation that promotes teamwork and collaboration in a cross-functional environment. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: Working remotely from home or any other work location Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Lead Engineer, Mathematics & Data Science, Research and Technology Development

# Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Posted Jun 8, 2026

Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Experience: 12–15+ Years (Data Science / AI Experience)

Employment Type: Full-Time (W2 Only)

### Location: USA (Hybrid / Onsite)

Work Authorization: H4 EAD, L2S, GC EAD, Green Card, US Citizen

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly accomplished Lead Data Scientist with strong Generative AI expertise to design and drive enterprise-scale AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in machine learning, statistical modeling, Large Language Models (LLMs), and advanced analytics, along with strong leadership capabilities to guide teams and deliver impactful AI-driven products.

This role requires ownership of GenAI solution design, model development, experimentation, deployment, and performance optimization across enterprise environments.

- Key Responsibilities
- Define and lead GenAI and data science strategy across business use cases.
- Design and implement LLM-based applications including RAG architectures.
- Lead development of machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions.
- Perform deep exploratory data analysis (EDA) and derive actionable insights.
- Architect end-to-end ML pipelines (data ingestion → training → deployment → monitoring).
- Optimize models for accuracy, scalability, and cost efficiency.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, and Product stakeholders.
- Establish best practices for MLOps, model governance, and evaluation frameworks.
- Mentor junior data scientists and lead technical decision-making.
- Drive innovation in AI/ML and Generative AI solutions.

Required Technical Skills

- Data Science & ML
- Strong expertise in Machine Learning & Statistical Modeling
- Python (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn)
- Feature engineering, model evaluation, and tuning

- Generative AI
- LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, LLaMA, etc.)
- Prompt engineering and LLM orchestration
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
- LangChain / LlamaIndex / Hugging Face

- Data & Processing
- SQL
- Large-scale data handling and transformation

- Cloud & MLOps
- AWS / Azure / GCP
- MLflow / SageMaker / Azure ML
- CI/CD for ML pipelines
- Model deployment and monitoring

- Preferred Qualifications
- Experience with Deep Learning (TensorFlow / PyTorch)
- Exposure to vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Chroma)
- Experience with Databricks or big data platforms
- Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills

- Eligibility & Compliance
- W2 Full-Time Only
- No C2C
- No consultancy or vendor profiles

How to Apply

Share your updated resume to:

manish.g@precisiontechcorp.com

Apply for This Job

results.title: Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
highlights:
Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
[...]
Senior Data Scientist
[...]
🦸‍♀️ The Role 🦸‍♀️
[...]
Our mission is to help large successful brands like Uber, Amazon, Wise, HelloFresh (and more!) put their customers at the centre of everything they do. Using best-in-class tech in a fast-developing AI space, our Customer Experience Intelligence platform continuously analyses explicit and implicit feedback to enable our clients to identify what they should do next.
[...]
We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist to join the team and help build and ship the next generation of that stack.
[...]
Unlike many companies, we use our own custom models, specialised for customer feedback, across various parts of the stack: extraction, retrieval, reranking, summarisation, and sentiment analysis. We are also pragmatic and understand that the right solution can be a combination of off-the-shelf LLMs, bespoke fine-tuned models, and sometimes techniques that utilise no LLM at all.
[...]
Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models and agentic systems for customer feedback, including owning our custom fine-tuning pipelines. Run experiments end-to-end, track results rigorously, and make clear recommendations on what to ship, iterate, or retire.
[...]
Build and maintain LLM-powered features: retrieval pipelines, reranking systems, insight agents, data mining agents, and automated taxonomy generation.
[...]
Design and run robust evaluation frameworks: build test sets, define metrics, evaluate non-deterministic systems, handle class imbalance, and automate checkpoint comparisons.
[...]
Improve and extend semantic search and retrieval, evolving from embedding-based approaches toward more advanced methods.
[...]
Write production-quality code and collaborate closely with Engineering on productionisation, model serving, data pipelines, and monitoring.
[...]
Work with Product and Commercial teams to translate business needs into practical ML solutions, and support client evaluations and accuracy benchmarking.
[...]
Mentor team members
[...]
🧰 What you’ll need:
[...]
A deep working knowledge of transformer architectures.
[...]
Strong PyTorch skills, with the ability to write custom training loops, modify model architectures, and debug issues at the tensor level. Ideally, experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques such as LoRA
[...]
Extensive experience working with large-scale, messy real-world text data, including classification, extraction, embeddings, re-rankers, clustering, and search.
[...]
Experience in instruction fine-tuning and serving language models, familiarity with frameworks such as vLLM, DeepSpeed, or similar tools
[...]
A solid grounding in classical ML and statistics, and the judgement to choose simpler methods when they’re the right solution.
[...]
Practical experience building with GenAI and agentic patterns.
[...]
Technical curiosity and a keen interest in AI – a love of experimenting to make the most of available technology.
[...]
MSc/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computational Linguistics or a closely related STEM field.
[...]
Experience with reinforcement learning techniques, such as with verifiable reward (RLVR)
[...]
#machinelearning #machinelearningscientist #seniormachinelearningscientist #datascience #deeplearning #LLM #ML #data #AI #PyTorch

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

results.title: Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria, working closely with domain experts.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Architect and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, ensuring robustness, efficiency, and reproducibility across training and evaluation workflows.
[...]
As a Principal ML Scientist:
[...]
You help define the long-term technical roadmap for scientific reasoning models, identifying new opportunities and setting priorities across initiatives.
[...]
You architect new initiatives
[...]
technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.
[...]
You serve as a key technical authority for leadership, influencing how Genentech leverages generative AI to solve high-stakes problems in the therapeutic pipeline.
[...]
For Senior (SE6): 0 – 2+ years of industry or post-doc experience with a focus on deep learning.
[...]
For Principal (SE7):
[...]
demonstrated track record of technical leadership and project ownership.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Extensive experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including approaches to improve domain understanding, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Engineering: Strong software engineering skills and experience designing and operating large-scale or high-performance machine learning systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data) is highly valued but not required.
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary range for this position, based on the primary location of San Francisco, is $167,400 – 310,800 of hiring range for the Senior Scientist, and $194,000 – 360,400 for the Principal Scientist. For the primary of location of New York City, $160,100 – 297,300 for the Senior Scientist, and $185,600 – 344,600 for the Principal Scientist. Actual pay will be determined based on experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors permitted by law. A discretionary annual bonus may be available based on individual and Company performance. This position also qualifies for the benefits detailed at the link provided below.
[...]
Genentech is a biotechnology research company that specializes in genetic testing and personalized medicines.
